summary: Checks ordinary List elements against one inferred homogeneous type or an explicit expected type, including an explicit closed union; it never synthesizes a Union.
requires: the literal constructs immutable List<T> and is never context-rewritten to NumericArray
every numeric token is validated once by NumericLiteralAdmitted; '-' remains a separate AST prefix operator
without context, an empty literal is not inferable and only identical delegated or nonnumeric normalized element types are admitted

with a fixed contextual List<T>, a nonnumeric element's normalized type must equal T exactly; assignment compatibility, subtype conversion, variance, hidden conversion, clone, ownership weakening, Option insertion, and source-order preference do not count as equality
with fixed exact integer T, only PrefixExpr(-, UnsuffixedIntegerLiteral) may request a magnitude fact and form signed candidate -magnitude; an in-range candidate admits as T
an out-of-range signed candidate, including Int8 -129 or UInt8 -1, emits LIST_LITERAL_CONTEXT_INTEGER_OUT_OF_RANGE
the prefix-sign adapter performs no arbitrary constant folding, hidden widening, or expression rewrite
other numeric elements require NumericLiteralAdmitted to return exact T; suffix, ISize/USize, and Float32 rules remain delegated
there is no implicit integer/float, signed/unsigned, width-changing, nominal, union, or source-order-dependent join
preconditions: the literal constructs immutable List<T> and is never context-rewritten to NumericArray
the optional contextual List<T> target is fixed before element normalization
numeric token parts, exact contextual target, and direct PrefixExpr shape are preserved
no hidden conversion, constant folding, synthesized union, or source-order preference has occurred
normalization: normalize exact List target and element AST shape; keep prefix sign separate from the numeric token
decision_procedure: validate the closed RCTS-V5 descriptor
for a direct prefix-minus unsuffixed integer under exact integer T, request NumericLiteralAdmitted magnitude-only mode, compute -magnitude once, and admit exact T iff representable
emit LIST_LITERAL_CONTEXT_INTEGER_OUT_OF_RANGE for an unrepresentable signed candidate; do not fold any other expression or insert conversion
otherwise evaluate NumericLiteralAdmitted once with contextual T when present and propagate its selected diagnostic
evaluate ListLiteralElementJoinAdmitted diagnostic_dispatch conditions once in their declared deterministic order
emit only the diagnostic selected by the first matching condition (or its declared advisory lint), never the entire secondary_diagnostics list; otherwise return success
termination_metric: finite descriptor size plus strictly decreasing dependency DAG rank
evaluation_order: preconditions -> normalization -> dependencies -> local rule -> diagnostic

summary: a value-producing @if has an else branch; the optional grammar tail is recovery-only; exactly one expression is admitted directly; otherwise every reachable normal path ends in local ret; return is an enclosing-function transfer and finally never creates the local value
requires: a value-producing @if has an else branch; the optional grammar tail is recovery-only
exactly one expression is admitted directly
otherwise every reachable normal path ends in local ret
return is an enclosing-function transfer and finally never creates the local value
preconditions: a value-producing @if has an else branch; the optional grammar tail is recovery-only
exactly one expression is admitted directly
otherwise every reachable normal path ends in local ret
return is an enclosing-function transfer and finally never creates the local value
normalization: normalize aliases, ownership regions, rows, labels, and witness identities before decision
decision_procedure: validate the closed RCTS-V5 descriptor
evaluate dependency predicates left-to-right in listed order

apply LocalValueBodyAdmitted local rule: a value-producing @if has an else branch; the optional grammar tail is recovery-only; exactly one expression is admitted directly; otherwise every reachable normal path ends in local ret; return is an enclosing-function transfer and finally never creates the local value
on local-rule failure emit active_primary_diagnostic; secondary_diagnostics are trace alternatives, not a batch emission list
termination_metric: finite descriptor size plus strictly decreasing dependency DAG rank
evaluation_order: preconditions -> normalization -> dependencies -> local rule -> diagnostic

summary: one closed intrinsic logical domain: List/String/Bytes and default NumericArray axes are one-based, bounded owners retain L..U, Map uses exact K, and ReadonlyView preserves its source owner's domain
requires: the receiver family is intrinsic rather than conformance-activated
the supplied index type and value are explicit
the declared logical bounds or exact Map key domain are available
preconditions: the receiver family and logical index-domain kind are uniquely normalized
no negative-from-end, hidden narrowing, truthiness, or witness rewrite has occurred
storage offsets are not source-visible inputs
normalization: normalize the intrinsic receiver family, source-owner identity, exact key/axis type, logical lower/upper bounds, shape and typed axes; ReadonlyView inherits its owner's domain and coordinates; never normalize source coordinates to offsets before admission
decision_procedure: validate the closed descriptor and select exactly one intrinsic domain; a ReadonlyView uses its source owner's declared domain and provenance; reject conformance-only or unknown receivers with LOGICAL_INDEX_DOMAIN_MISMATCH
require exact Int for positional/bounded/NumericArray coordinates and exact K for Map; reject every wrong key or axis type with LOGICAL_INDEX_DOMAIN_MISMATCH

List/String/Bytes and their direct views use 1..length; bounded or sliced owners and their views preserve declared/selected coordinates; each default NumericArray axis uses typed Int 1..dimension without a Sequence witness
reject zero with ZERO_BASED_INDEX_NOT_CURRENT only when it is outside a default one-based positional or NumericArray axis; Map key 0 and preserved domains containing 0 remain admissible
reject a statically known out-of-domain coordinate with INDEX_OUT_OF_LOGICAL_DOMAIN; admit a type-correct dynamic coordinate with runtime check IndexError::outOfLogicalDomain before access
only after static admission or installing that runtime check derive internal index-lower_bound while retaining owner provenance and source-visible coordinates
termination_metric: finite descriptor size plus strictly decreasing dependency DAG rank
evaluation_order: preconditions -> normalization -> dependencies -> local rule -> diagnostic

summary: ordered branch 1: a value-carrying break without an immediately following outcome match emits BREAK_VALUE_REQUIRES_LOOP_OUTCOME_MATCH; ordered branch 2: a present outcome match using a pattern other than the admitted outcome cases emits LOOP_OUTCOME_MATCH_REQUIRES_OUTCOME_CASE; ordered branch 3: a present correctly spelled match that omits any reachable outcome emits LOOP_OUTCOME_MATCH_NON_EXHAUSTIVE; only a present, correctly spelled, exhaustive outcome match admits the value-carrying loop result
requires: ordered branch 1: a value-carrying break without an immediately following outcome match emits BREAK_VALUE_REQUIRES_LOOP_OUTCOME_MATCH
ordered branch 2: a present outcome match using a pattern other than the admitted outcome cases emits LOOP_OUTCOME_MATCH_REQUIRES_OUTCOME_CASE
ordered branch 3: a present correctly spelled match that omits any reachable outcome emits LOOP_OUTCOME_MATCH_NON_EXHAUSTIVE
only a present, correctly spelled, exhaustive outcome match admits the value-carrying loop result

evaluate dependency predicates left-to-right and propagate the dependency's selected diagnostic unchanged
evaluate LoopOutcomeExhaustive diagnostic_dispatch conditions once in their declared deterministic order
